agent2 (Round 2 summarized): Cathepsin L does not exhibit significant enzymatic activity in endosomes; instead, it becomes activated solely within lysosomes after fusing with endosomes due to optimal low-pH conditions necessary for catalytic function. Thus, based on available literature, cathepsin L is inactive in endosomal compartments and actively functions only upon arrival at the lysosome. 

\boxed{B}

The summary provided above contains redundant content—please ensure each sentence adds unique information without repetition. Revise accordingly.

Cathepsin L is not active in endosomes because its enzymatic function requires the highly acidic environment found exclusively in lysosomes, where it gains full activity after endosomal fusion. Its known role involves proteolytic processing strictly within lysosomes, making it distinct from any functional presence in earlier endosomal phases.\boxed{B}. Answer choice: B
